channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Meteorological Authority Warns of Heavy Rainfall Across Several Regions
item.description: The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has issued a weather alert warning of heavy rainfall expected to affect several regions in Tanzania. On March 20, 2026, heavy rains are forecasted for Dar e...

item.title: Over a thousand cats and dogs in Bulacan spayed and neutered for rabies awareness
item.description: In observance of Rabies Awareness Month, the provincial government of Bulacan organized a mass spaying and neutering project that benefited 1,467 cats and dogs across the province. The activity took p...

item.title: Expensive driver’s licences limit job opportunities for youth in Brits
item.description: In Brits, North West, the steep cost of obtaining a driver’s licence—ranging from R5,000 for those with some experience to R10,000 for beginners—is increasingly excluding young people from employment ...

item.title: Australia's Quiet Adoption of Palantir Software and Its Implications for Democracy
item.description: In March 2026, Palantir Technologies embedded itself within Australia’s intelligence and financial systems under the guise of a software upgrade. This system update, which bypassed parliamentary debat...

item.title: Impact of the Iran War on Global Energy and U.S. Policy
item.description: The article from Sky News explores the global fallout from escalating hostilities between Iran and Israel, particularly following two major attacks targeting vital energy plants in the Middle East. On...

item.title: Senate Republicans Claim Democratic Resistance Is Driven by Far-Left Pressure During DHS Shutdown
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune, a Republican from South Dakota, suggested that the ongoing Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown is being prolonged because Senate Democrats are pressured b...

item.title: Concerns rise as DOH official’s brother secures P141-M health infrastructure contracts
item.description: Rappler’s March 20, 2026, edition of The wRap highlights several key stories from the Philippines and abroad. A major report reveals that Erni Baggao, the brother of Department of Health Undersecretar...

item.title: Ladakh’s Tourism Sector Faces Setbacks Due to Multiple Disruptions in 2025
item.description: Ladakh’s tourism industry faced significant challenges in 2025, causing considerable economic stress for local businesses. Following a surge in tourism in 2022 and 2023 after the easing of COVID-19 re...

item.title: Durban Residents Seek Class Action Against UPL Over Toxic Pesticide Fire
item.description: A group of residents from Durban has approached the KwaZulu-Natal Division of the High Court seeking permission to launch a class action lawsuit against UPL, the Indian agrochemicals company, for alle...

item.title: Cuba Dismisses U.S. Proposal to Discuss Leadership Change Amid Economic Tensions
item.description: Cuba has firmly rejected any suggestion from the United States that its political system or the term of President Miguel Díaz-Canel could be subject to negotiation. The response followed reports that ...

item.title: Nasir El-Rufai Mourns Death of Sister-in-Law, Safiya Ali Rufai
item.description: Former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, is currently dealing with a heavy blow as he mourns the passing of his sister-in-law, Safiya Ali Rufai. The news of her death was announced by his brother...

item.title: Arteta confident as Arsenal prepare to face Manchester City in Carabao Cup final
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has expressed confidence in his team’s readiness as they prepare to face Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final at Wembley Stadium. The Spaniard urged his side to 'attac...

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Rejects Involvement in Oman and Turkiye Attacks, Blames Israel for ‘False Flag’ Operations
item.description: Iran’s new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, firmly denied on Friday any Iranian role in the recent attacks that took place in Turkiye and Oman, describing them as a 'false flag tactic' allegedly orch...

item.title: Exploring Female Loneliness and Complex Friendships in Asako Yuzuki’s 'Hooked'
item.description: Asako Yuzuki’s novel 'Hooked', translated into English by Polly Barton, examines the intricate dynamics of female loneliness and obsession in contemporary Tokyo. The story focuses on two women in thei...

item.title: Jon Stewart and Elon Musk Engage in Public Dispute Over Social Media and Democracy
item.description: This week, comedian Jon Stewart and billionaire Elon Musk engaged in a very public clash over social media and its impact on American democracy. The exchange began when Stewart aired a segment on “The...

item.title: Allans Ademba Leads Youth Voter Registration Campaign 'Uko Kadi?'
item.description: Kenyan youth are increasingly refusing to stay on the sidelines when it comes to politics, and the 'Uko Kadi?' movement is a perfect example. The campaign, started by 26-year-old photojournalist Allan...

item.title: Carhartt’s Midweight Pocket Tee Stands Out for Quality, Comfort, and Durability
item.description: HuffPost highlights a seasonal sale on Carhartt’s midweight short-sleeved pocket T-shirt, a long-standing favorite among shoppers for its quality, comfort, and longevity. Currently priced at around $2...

item.title: TESDA offers free skills assessment and certification for senior high school graduates
item.description: The Technical Education and Skills Development Authority (TESDA) has announced that over 260,000 senior high school (SHS) students under the Technical-Vocational-Livelihood (TVL) track will benefit fr...

item.title: Actor Chuck Norris Dies at 86
item.description: American action star Chuck Norris, renowned for his roles in films like 'Code of Silence,' 'Missing in Action,' 'The Delta Force,' and the iconic television series 'Walker, Texas Ranger,' has died at ...

item.title: Centre Reviews Farm Losses After Unseasonal Rains and Hailstorms Across India
item.description: On 20 March 2026, the Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan chaired a high-level meeting in New Delhi to review the impact of recent unseasonal rains, hailstorms, and adverse weather condit...
